title: ebenus reesei subsp. minor
intro: Ebenus reesei subsp. minor, commonly known as the Minor Spur Violet, is a perennial plant species in the family Papaveraceae, characterized by its small, delicate purple flowers and heart-shaped leaves. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly as an antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent, making it beneficial for treating wounds and soothing skin irritations. The Minor Spur Violet is also valued for its health benefits, including its antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, which can help to boost the immune system and protect against infections. In herbal preparations, Ebenus reesei subsp. minor is often used in infusions, teas, and topical creams to harness its medicinal and therapeutic properties.
